objective: Real GPT-5.6 high-reasoning new-game blueprint rehearsal
inputIdea: A keyboard-controlled top-down arena where the player taps space to emit a pulse that pushes nearby enemies away. The fun should come from creating space at the last moment. The smallest playable result is one player, one enemy, and one repeatable push in a bounded arena.

validatedBlueprintSummary.vision: A compact Godot 4 2D top-down arena prototype where precise keyboard movement and a well-timed pulse let the player reclaim space from an approaching enemy.
validatedBlueprintSummary.coreAction: Tap Space to emit a short-range pulse that pushes nearby enemies away from the player.
validatedBlueprintSummary.funTarget: Create breathing room at the last possible moment as an enemy closes in.
validatedBlueprintSummary.smallestPlayableResult: One keyboard-controlled player, one pursuing enemy, and one repeatable push pulse inside a bounded arena.
validatedBlueprintSummary.firstPlayableMilestone: The player can move around a bounded arena, allow one enemy to approach, and repeatedly use a clearly readable pulse to knock it back before contact.

quests.title: Build the Bounded Arena
quests.visibleOutcome: A simple top-down arena appears with clear code-native walls and a distinct player shape that stays within the play area.

quests.title: Add Responsive Player Movement
quests.visibleOutcome: The player moves smoothly in four directions with the keyboard and stops predictably when input is released.

quests.title: Create the Pursuing Enemy
quests.visibleOutcome: One visually distinct enemy steadily approaches the player while remaining constrained by the arena.

quests.title: Implement the Push Pulse
quests.visibleOutcome: Pressing Space produces a brief visible pulse around the player and pushes the nearby enemy directly away.

quests.title: Tune the Last-Moment Loop
quests.visibleOutcome: The enemy closes distance at a readable pace, the pulse has a repeatable rhythm, and successful late pushes create a satisfying moment of regained space.
